mission.title: Install and Configure Carina on Kubernetes
mission.description: Carina is a high-performance, ops-free local storage solution for Kubernetes, designed specifically for cloud-native databases. Installing Carina allows you to leverage its capabilities for efficient storage management and low-latency performance in your Kubernetes environment.

steps.title: Add the Carina Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Carina Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add carina https://carina-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Carina using Helm
steps.description:
Install Carina with the following command, creating a namespace called 'carina' if it doesn't exist:
```bash
helm install carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --create-namespace --version v0.11.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Carina pods are running successfully:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace carina
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits for Carina components
steps.description:
Update the Helm release to set resource limits for the Carina CSI driver components:
```bash
helm upgrade carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --set controller.resources.limits.cpu=200m --set controller.resources.limits.memory=500Mi
```

steps.title: Set up RBAC for Carina
steps.description:
Ensure that the necessary RBAC permissions are created by upgrading the Helm release:
```bash
helm upgrade carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --set rbac.create=true
```

steps.title: Check Carina health status
steps.description:
Use port-forwarding to access the health check endpoint:
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/csi-carina-controller --namespace carina 8080:8080
# Then visit http://localhost:8080/healthz in your browser to check the health status.
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Carina will result in the Carina pods running in the 'carina' namespace. You can verify the health of the installation by accessing the health check endpoint and ensuring that all components are operational.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add carina https://carina-io.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --create-namespace --version v0.11.0
helm upgrade carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --set controller.resources.limits.cpu=200m --set controller.resources.limits.memory=500Mi
helm upgrade carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --set rbac.create=true

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Carina Helm release to remove the deployment from your cluster. This will delete all associated resources managed by Helm.
```bash
helm uninstall carina --namespace carina
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources are removed from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep carina | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace carina
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'carina' namespace to clean up any remaining resources. Verify that the namespace has been removed successfully.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace carina
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's important to back up the current state of your Carina installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
helm get values carina --namespace carina > carina-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to ensure you have the latest charts, then upgrade the Carina installation to the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade carina carina/carina-csi-driver --namespace carina --version v0.12.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the Carina pods to ensure they are running the new version without issues.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If you see that the Carina pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs of the affected pod for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Persistent Volume Claims not bound
troubleshooting.description:
If PVCs are not bound, ensure that the storage class is correctly configured and available. Check the PVC status with:
```bash
kubectl get pvc --namespace carina
```

troubleshooting.title: Health check endpoint returns error
troubleshooting.description:
If the health check endpoint is returning an error, verify that the Carina controller is running and check its logs for any issues.
```bash
kubectl logs deployment/csi-carina-controller --namespace carina
```

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permissions issues
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter permission errors, ensure that the RBAC roles and bindings are correctly set up. Check the roles with:
```bash
kubectl get roles --namespace carina
kubectl get rolebindings --namespace carina
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or later, and that Helm and kubectl are installed and configured on your local machine.
